title: Brake kinetic energy recovery system
abstract: The brake kinetic energy recovery system, according to the invention, consists of the motor hydraulic pumps (A and A 1 ) which are mounted on the wheels of the vehicle, a distributor (B), of the hydraulic tanks (C and D) in connection with the air tanks (40 and 46), a pneumohydraulic cylinder (E), an oil tank (24) connected to an air tank (26), the control thereof being performed by means of the distributors (3, 3') provided with the double circuit solenoid valves (18, 19) and of the solenoid valves (28 and 29), assemblies that are connected to each other by means of the lines (16, 17, 22, 23, 25, 27, 36 37, 39, 41, 42, 45, 48, 50, 51, 53, 54 and 55). The motor hydraulic pumps (A and A 1 ) consist of the rotors (1), the stators (2) and the distributors (3 and 3'), the latter being coupled together, the hydraulic connection between the distributors (3 and 3') and the stators (2) being performed by the lines (16 and 17). The stator (2) and the flange (13) are provided with the circular channels (g) in connection with some radial channels (h) which are coupled with a cross channel (i), whereas any leakage of oil between the stator (2) and the rotor (1) can be disposed through the exit channel (j).
claims: Claims Claims 1. Brake kinetic energy recovery system, characterized in that it consists of motor hydraulic pumps (A and Ai) which are mounted on the wheels of the vehicle, a distributor (B), the hydraulic tanks (C and D) being in connection with air tanks (40 and 46), a pneumohydraulic cylinder (E), from an oil tank (24) connected to an air tank (26), the control thereof being performed by means of distributors (3 and 3') equipped with double circuit solenoid valves (18 and 19) and of solenoid valves (28 and 29), assemblies that are connected to each other by means of lines (16, 17, 22, 23, 25 27, 36, 37, 39, 41, 42, 45, 48, 50, 51, 53, 54 and 55). 2. Brake kinetic energy recovery system, according to claim 1, characterized in that the motor hydraulic pumps (A and Ai) are formed of the rotors (1), the stators (2) and the distributors (3, 3'), the latter being coupled together, a hydraulic connection between the distributors (3, 3') and the stators (2) being achieved by the lines (16 and 17). 3. Brake kinetic energy recovery system, according to claim 1 and 2, characterized in that the stator (2) and the flange (13) have circular channels (g) connected to radial channels (h) that are coupled with a cross channel (i), whereas any leakage of oil between the stator (2) and the rotor (1) can be disposed through the exit channel (j). 4. Brake kinetic energy recovery system, according to claim 1 , characterized in that the distributor (B) is composed of a tubular element (31) which has threaded holes (ri, n and n) and another threaded hole (s), whereas on the tubular element (31) a metallic casing (32) is mounted, whereas inside the tubular member (31) a piston (33) is sliding and has the circular channels (ti, t2 and t3) in which the oil passes to the threaded holes (ri, Î2 and ra) and with other circular channels (u) and the piston (33) is pressed by a helical spring (34) resting on said tubular element (31), and an electromagnet (35) is attached to the distributor (B) and the metal rod of the electromagnet is designed to block the piston (33). 5. Brake kinetic energy recovery system, according to claim 1 , characterized in that the pneumohydraulic cylinder (E) is made from tubular elements (wi and w 2 ) with a piston (x) sliding axially within them and an electromagnet (w3) which in turn may drive the piston (x), the tubular element (wi) being provided with a threaded hole (zi), and the tubular element (w 2 ) with a threaded hole (z 2 ).
